item.title: The Lakers' summer dilemma after another early playoff exit
item.description: LeBron James said the right things after Game 5, but the questions about the Lakers' direction are no longer hypothetical.
item.content:encoded:
LeBron James said the right things after Game 5, but the questions about the Lakers' direction are no longer hypothetical. Sources around the league expect Rob Pelinka to explore several paths that would have been unthinkable two seasons ago.

Anthony Davis is the most obvious lever.

item.title: Long-form: a deep look at the Celtics' switching defense and how it broke Cleveland
item.description: A look inside Joe Mazzulla's defensive scheme.
item.content:encoded:
The Boston Celtics played 32 minutes of switching defense in the second half of Game 4 against the Cleveland Cavaliers and the on-ball pressure broke every action Cleveland tried to run. The numbers underneath the result are even more striking. Jaylen Brown defended 14 separate ball-handlers across those 32 minutes and held them to a combined 4-for-19. Jrue Holiday took the toughest assignment whenever Cleveland tried to put Donovan Mitchell in a pick-and-roll. The result was a defensive possession length that crept above 18 seconds for nearly every Cavs trip down the floor and an offensive rating that collapsed from 118 in the first half to 84 in the second. This is the kind of half that turns a series.

Continued for many more paragraphs about rotation timing, screen navigation, and the specific reads Mazzulla taught his guards during the offseason.

item.title: Notebook: Wemby 🦄 has changed the math for the Spurs
item.description: Victor Wembanyama's second year has San Antonio recalibrating its timeline.
item.content:encoded: Victor Wembanyama's second year has the San Antonio Spurs recalibrating their timeline. League executives polled this week put the Spurs in the “sooner than expected” column for playoff contention.

item.title: Old: a thought from last week on the trade deadline
Brief: notes from around the league
